oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with feature-specific fields) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: the exact scope_selector string passed in the test input, e.g., '#header or '.header\'="
the exact class name string passed in the test input, e.g., 'btn-primary' or 'active'
the exact CSS selector string passed in the test input, e.g., '.status-text' or 'p:last-child'"

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_text_presence, feature2_selector_presence, feature3_scoped_text_presence, feature4_attribute_match, feature5_class_presence, feature6_link_target_presence, feature7_ordered_selection_text, feature8_reusable_charset_check. Feature descriptions include: Checks whether raw rendered markup includes a required text fragment, including fragments inside complete documents, multiple root nodes, and recovered malformed markup.; Checks whether a rendered document, or a scoped fragment of it, includes at least one element matching a CSS selector.; Narrows a document to elements matching a CSS selector and then checks that the selected fragment contains a required text fragment.; Checks whether the root element of the current document or selected fragment has a named attribute with an exact expected value.; Checks whether the current document or selected fragment contains an element carrying a required CSS class token.; Checks whether the current document or selected fragment contains an anchor element whose href exactly matches a required target.; Selects the first, last, or zero-based indexed element from a selector result and checks that the selected element contains a required text fragment.; Allows a reusable custom check to validate that a document head begins with a metadata element declaring an exact character set.
The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: document=['

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same scope application pattern as used in the root attribute matcher
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Apply the 'scope_selector' to the document root first, then apply the 'selection_selector' to the resulting set, respecting 'selection_mode' and 'position' indices.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the exact attribute name convention defined in the metadata parser utility
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The term 'name' inside '<meta>' may be 'charset', 'http-equiv', or 'name' depending on the parser; require the agent to check the standard <head> parser implementation.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 1 endpoint receives 'text' key, no 'scope_selector' provided.
_constraints.output_rule: Check if literal 'text' string exists anywhere within the raw 'document' string (including across root nodes and malformed markup).

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 2 endpoint receives 'selector' key; optionally receives 'scope_selector'.
_constraints.output_rule: Parse document, optionally scope to 'scope_selector' root elements, then check if any element matches 'selector'.
Parse document, extract all text content from elements matching 'scope_selector', then verify 'text' exists within that extracted fragment.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 4 endpoint receives 'attribute' and 'value' keys; optionally requests 'scope_selector', 'selection_selector', 'selection_mode', 'position'.
_constraints.output_rule: Apply scope and selection filters to identify a target element. Retrieve the named attribute value from the ROOT of that selected element (if selection returns multiple nodes, behavior implies checking the root of the result set or the root of the matches; test cases imply checking the root of the final selected fragment). Compare to 'value' exactly.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 5 endpoint receives 'class' key; optionally receives 'scope_selector', 'selection_selector', 'selection_mode', 'position'.
_constraints.output_rule: Parse document, filter elements by scope/selection options, then verify any element in the result set (or specifically the selected element if index/first is fixed) contains the 'class' token in its 'class' attribute.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 6 endpoint receives 'href' key; optionally receives 'scope_selector', 'selection_selector', 'selection_mode'.
_constraints.output_rule: Parse document, filter by scope/selection options, then verify any anchor matching 'selection_selector' (or root if not specified) within the result set has an 'href' attribute equal to 'href' exactly.

_constraints.input_condition: Feature 7 endpoint receives 'selection_selector', 'selection_mode' ('first', 'last', or number), and 'text'.
_constraints.output_rule: Filter elements by 'selection_selector'. If 'selection_mode' is 'first' or 'last', select that single element. If a number, select the element at that index. Verify 'text' exists inside the selected element.
Parse document. Verify that a <meta> tag exists with the name attribute (case-insensitive? likely exact 'charset') set to the provided 'charset' value (case-sensitive based on test case 'utf-8').
